tasks.description: Fix quality gate score thresholds in auto-approval prompt
tasks.context: Update SYSTEM_PROMPT in src/agents/minimax/system-prompt.ts to enforce correct thresholds: complexity ≤7, maintainability ≥65, test_coverage ≥80. Currently allows approval with failing scores. Add explicit rejection logic for infrastructure changes without ADR.

tasks.description: Add Polygon mainnet support to x402 payment protocol
tasks.context: Extend src/lib/x402.ts to support chainId 137 (Polygon). Add USDC contract address 0x3c499c542cEF5E3811e1192ce70d8cC03d5c3359. Maintain existing Ethereum support. Update type definitions for multi-chain.

issues.description: Environment variables (ETHEREUM_RPC_URL, POLYGON_RPC_URL) default to empty strings which could cause runtime issues if not set
Missing JSDoc examples for complex functions like parsePaymentHeaders
Could benefit from explicit validation that RPC URLs are set before use

strengths: Proper TypeScript strict typing with no 'any' types used
Comprehensive Zod validation schemas for all payment headers
Clean separation of concerns with dedicated interfaces and utility functions
Maintains backward compatibility while extending functionality
Good error handling with descriptive error messages
Proper use of const assertions and type guards
Well-documented with JSDoc comments on all public APIs
Follows project architecture patterns with proper exports
Correct Polygon USDC contract address as specified
Type-safe chain ID validation and supported chain checking
No invalid syntax or markdown fences in source code
Zod validation schema correctly added for chainId 137 and other headers
Type definitions (TokenConfig, ChainConfig, SupportedChainId) are clear and type-safe
Helper functions (getChainConfig, getUsdcConfig, isSupportedChain) follow project patterns
Maintains compatibility with Ethereum mainnet and cleanly introduces Polygon support
No hardcoded secrets or insecure patterns

tasks.description: Register Polygon network in chain configuration
tasks.context: Add Polygon mainnet to src/config/chains.ts with chainId 137, RPC endpoints, block explorer. Include USDC token config. Ensure backward compatibility with existing Ethereum config.

review.summary: Dual-rejected: Claude (25/100) + Codex (25/100). File is incomplete - cuts off mid-implementation without the critical chainMap export and utility functions. Missing test file entirely. Several type safety and architectural issues. | The new Polygon config was added, but the file is incomplete and contains type mismatches that will break compilation/runtime validation.

issues.description: File is incomplete - cuts off at line 'Map of chain ID to chain configuration' without implementing the chainMap export or utility functions
No test file provided - missing __tests__/chains.test.ts entirely
Type safety issue: blockExplorer uses 'new URL()' constructor but schema expects string - runtime type mismatch
RPC array filtering with 'filter(Boolean) as string[]' is unsafe - could include non-string values if env vars are not strings
Missing JSDoc documentation for exported types and main configuration objects
No error handling for invalid environment variables or malformed URLs
Hardcoded oracle heartbeat values without configuration flexibility
The file is truncated. The ‘Map of chain ID to chain configuration’ section is started but not implemented or exported, leading to a syntax error.
blockExplorer is declared in the Zod schema as z.string().url(), but the code assigns a URL object (new URL(...)) instead of a string.
The comment for the Polygon Chainlink feed references USDC/USD but then labels it MATIC/USD. Clarify or correct the comment and ensure the correct feed address is used.
Although Zod schemas are defined, the code never runs zod.parse() or validation on the exported configs, reducing the effectiveness of the schema definitions.

review.summary: Dual-rejected: Claude (25/100) + Codex (40/100). File is incomplete and truncated, missing critical CSS content. Contains syntax error at end. | The globals.css file has been updated with the new brand palette and dark mode variants correctly, but the file is truncated (cut off at ‘font-fea’) and missing closing braces, resulting in invalid CSS. Please provide the complete file.

issues.description: File is truncated - ends abruptly with 'font-fea' which is invalid CSS syntax
Missing complete body styling and potentially other CSS rules that were cut off
No contrast ratio validation provided - task specifically required following brand guidelines for contrast ratios
Dark mode primary colors appear incorrect - lighter values assigned to higher numbered variants (600-900) which is backwards
File appears truncated at “font-fea”, missing closing braces and remaining layer/base definitions. This will break CSS parsing.
